data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Charles Schwab beats Q3 top-line and bottom-line estimates
Dragonfly Energy prices $55.4M public offering; shares down
data.description:
• Dragonfly Energy (NASDAQ:DFLI) priced an underwritten public offering of 36M common shares at $1.35/share and 5M pre-funded warrants at $1.3499 each.
  • The offering is expected to raise ~$55.4M in gross proceeds before expenses.
  • A 30-day option has been granted to underwriters to purchase up to 6.15M additional shares.
  • All securities in the offering are being sold by the company itself.
  • Net proceeds will be used for working capital, $45M debt prepayment, and strategic investment in next-gen battery tech, including dry electrode and solid-state battery scaling.
  • Offering is expected to close on or about October 17, 2025.
  • DFLI shares down 13.7% premarket on Thursday.

data.title: MGM Resorts sells the operations of MGM Northfield Park for $546M
data.description:
MGM Resorts International (NYSE:MGM) announced on Thursday that it has reached an agreement to sell the operations of MGM Northfield Park to private equity funds managed by Clairvest Group Inc. The deal was struck for $546 million in cash, subject to customary purchase price adjustments.

MGM Northfield Park is a racino (combined racetrack and casino) and entertainment complex located in Northfield, Ohio, between Cleveland and Akron. It is owned by VICI Properties (VICI) and operated by MGM Resorts International (NYSE:MGM).

MGM Growth Properties LLC acquired the Hard Rock Rocksino Northfield Park in July 2018. In 2019, MGM Resorts acquired the operations from MGP for $275 million plus purchase price adjustments and rebranded the property as MGM Northfield Park. 

For the twelve months ended June 30, MGM Northfield Park reported adjusted EBITDAR of approximately $137 million. At the closing of the transaction, MGM Resorts' (MGM) master lease agreement with VICI Properties (VICI), which currently includes MGM Northfield Park, will be amended to reduce annual rent by $54 million. The company expects estimated net cash proceeds after taxes and transaction costs to be approximately $420 million. 

"This is an excellent result for MGM Resorts and demonstrates consistency in driving transaction multiples at meaningful premiums over where MGM Resorts currently trades. The divestiture underscores MGM Resorts' exceptional financial stewardship, delivering substantial value well beyond the original acquisition price," stated MGM Resorts CFO Jonathan Halkyard. "We appreciate VICI, as the real estate owner of MGM Northfield Park, working constructively with Clairvest to facilitate a new lease agreement," he added.

The transaction is expected to close in the first half of 2026.

Shares of MGM Resorts (MGM) rose 2.3% in premarket trading, while VICI Properties (VICI) traded flat.

data.title: Will consumers ramp holiday spending to scare off ghosts of Christmas future?
data.description:
U.S. consumers might be facing a chill in sentiments this holiday season owing to a cooling jobs market, tariff fears, and a decline in confidence. However, Wells Fargo believes that will not stop them from engaging in some retail therapy.

Wells Fargo expects holiday sales to rise between 3.5% and 4% over last year, as consumers look for some comfort and normalcy amid economic uncertainties. As per the note, when people feel down, they are more likely to spend, especially during holidays, also known as retail therapy. 

However, signs of tariff pressures will be all over the holidays this year, and gifts are likely to be more expensive, according to Wells Fargo's economist Tim Quinlan.

The current forecast of 3.5%-4% for holiday sales is below the longer-run average of 4.7% registered since 1992 and also below the 4.3% average over the prior expansion from 2010 to 2019, said Wells Fargo, adding, "That is because we anticipate a slight slowing in sales growth in the fourth quarter amid tighter budgets, higher prices and some signs of consumer restraint."

As per the note, "holiday sales" refers to the total retail spending during November and December, excluding spending at auto and parts dealers, gas stations and bars and restaurants.  

"Not only is the holiday-spending basket looking more expensive, but the prices of many holiday-related services are also up big, eating into the ability of consumers to spend more on traditional gifts this season," Quinlan said. 

Shoppers' behavior has also shifted, as consumers have pulled forward their purchases of discretionary goods earlier this year amid tariff concerns. Higher prices could also mean that gift certificates are the "it" gift of the year, especially for budget-conscious customers who are looking to tighten their purse strings and will want to stick to a specific dollar amount, Quinlan added.

There has also been a broader secular shift toward online shopping, which the tariff fears have only boosted further. The trend, lifted by e-commerce events like Prime Day, has impacted the traditional December sales spike.

"This could be another headwind to holiday sales, but it would also come with a boost to sales in early 2026 as gift cards get marked as purchases at time of redemption,"  Quinlan noted. 

Weaker wage gains, particularly for lower earners, may lead to consumers opting to pay through credit cards and buy-now, pay-later services.

Quinlan added that December is not as special as it used to be, but the consumer is making a final push to the finish line, even though the terrain ahead is rough. 

Here are some Consumer Discretionary ETFs: (NYSEARCA:XLY), (VCR), (FXD), (FDIS), (RSPD), and (RXI).

Here are some Consumer Staples ETFs: (NYSEARCA:XLP), (VDC), (IYK), (FSTA), (KXI), and (RSPS).

data.title: Idaho Strategic Resources files mixed shelf offering; size not disclosed
data.description:
• Idaho Strategic Resources (NYSE:IDR) has filed a shelf prospectus allowing it to offer and sell various securities over time.
  • Potential offerings include common stock, senior and subordinated debt (convertible/exchangeable), warrants, rights, and units.

data.title: Praxis surges after late-stage trial success for movement disorder therapy
data.description:
Praxis Precision Medicines (NASDAQ:PRAX) added ~134% in the premarket on Thursday after the Boston-based biotech announced that its experimental therapy ulixacaltamide succeeded in two late-stage trials for essential tremor, the commonest type of movement disorder.

Based on data from its Phase 3 Essential3 program, which comprises two simultaneously enrolled trials, Praxis (NASDAQ:PRAX) said that the eight-week Study 1 hit the primary endpoint related to the change in a clinical measure known as mADL11.

According to topline data from Study 1, ulixacaltamide led to a statistically significant and clinically meaningful 4.3 point average improvement in the mADL11 score at Week 8 and achieved all secondary endpoints with statistical significance.

Study 2, which enrolled people who improved by 3 points in the mADL11, also hit the primary endpoint, as 55% of patients on ulixacaltamide maintained response compared to 33% in the placebo arm with a statistically significant effect.

As for safety, Praxis (NASDAQ:PRAX) noted that its experimental therapy was well-tolerated and added that there were no mortalities or treatment-related serious adverse events.

The company expects to submit a new drug application to the FDA in early 2026 to secure marketing approval for ulixacaltamide and request a pre-NDA meeting ahead of the filing.

data.title: Mako Mining announces C$50M bought deal and private placement financing
data.description:
• Mako Mining (MKO:CA, MAKOF) launched a C$35M bought deal private placement led by Stifel Canada and Cantor Fitzgerald Canada.
  • The offering includes 4.38M common shares at C$8.00/share.
  • Underwriters have a 15% over-allotment option, which could raise an additional C$5.25M, bringing the brokered total to C$40.25M.
  • Wexford Capital LP to subscribe for 1.88M shares in a C$15M non-brokered private placement.
  • Total gross proceeds from both offerings expected to be C$50M.
  • Funds will be used for ramp-up at Moss Mine, development projects, and general working capital.
  • Closing is expected by October 28, 2025.

data.title: European indexes hover around flatline as investors analyze political uncertainties
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 7.20am ET.

London (UKX) -0.13% at 9,412. UK's GDP moves higher, trade deficit widens.The British pound remained above $1.34 after UK GDP data came in line with forecasts.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.06% to 24,200.

France (CAC:IND) +0.70% to 8,133. France’s reappointed Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu has a chance of surviving two no-confidence votes in the National Assembly after suspending President Emmanuel Macron’s landmark pension reform to gain support from the Socialists. 

Europe's trade surplus drops to €1B in August.

In other parts of Europe, Producer prices in the Czech Republic decreased by 1.0% Y/Y in September.

Norway’s industrial confidence indicator fell to -0.3 in Q3.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) turned 0.01% lower to 567.7, pausing after gains in the previous session. Investors continued to await new catalysts while digesting a fresh batch of corporate earnings.

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury was down 2 basis points to 4.03%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 2.57%.

UK's 10-year yield was up less than 1 basis point to 4.55%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Charles Schwab Non-GAAP EPS of $1.31 beats by $0.06, revenue of $6.14B beats by $120M
data.description:
• Charles Schwab press release (NYSE:SCHW): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.31 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $6.14B (+26.6% Y/Y) beats by $120M.
  • Total client assets increased 17% year-over-year to a record $11.59 trillion
  • Core net new assets of $137.5 billion brings year-to-date asset gathering to $355.5 billion – up 41% year-over-year
  • New brokerage account openings exceeded 1 million for the 4th consecutive quarter, pushing active brokerage accounts and total client accounts to 38.0 million and 45.7 million, respectively
  • Managed Investing Solutions net inflows grew 40% versus 3Q24
  • Margin balances ended the quarter at $97.2 billion – up 16% versus year-end 2024
  • Daily average trading volume was 7.4 million – up 30% versus 3Q24
  • Shares +2.1% PM.
